description: Redesign the hidden project management panel into a professional Adobe-style file management overlay. Currently the panel is a minimal modal with basic text inputs. Transform it into a polished, production-grade project management dialog with: (1) a proper overlay/modal with blur backdrop, (2) tabbed navigation (New, Open, Settings), (3) project list with visual cards showing name/last-modified/workspace-count, (4) proper create-project form with name/description/settings, (5) project settings editor, (6) the access mechanism changed from Ctrl+Shift+P (which conflicts with browser print) to Ctrl+Shift+K plus triple-click on app logo (keep triple-click, also add the new keyboard shortcut). All settings remain hidden - no visible settings button/menu in the normal UI.
steps: Step 1 - Change Keyboard Shortcut: In the `handleSecretKey` event handler in src/App.jsx, change the keyboard shortcut from Ctrl+Shift+P to Ctrl+Shift+K. Keep the triple-click on logo functionality (handleLogoTap). Keep the boss key (Ctrl+Shift+/) unchanged.

Step 2 - Redesign Panel Structure: Replace the current `renderProjectPanel` function with a new Adobe-style overlay. The overlay should: (a) have a dark backdrop with blur, (b) use a larger modal (max-w-lg instead of max-w-xs), (c) have a left sidebar with navigation icons/tabs: 'New Project', 'Open Project', 'Project Settings', 'Export/Import', (d) have a main content area that changes based on selected tab, (e) have smooth enter/exit animations using Tailwind's animate utilities.

Step 3 - New Project Tab: Create a polished 'New Project' form inside the panel with: project name input (styled), optional description textarea, a password/key field with show/hide toggle, a confirm button with loading state. Add validation with inline error messages. The form should feel like Adobe's New Document dialog.

Step 4 - Open Project Tab: Create a project browser/switcher showing all projects as visual cards in a grid/list. Each card shows: project name, number of workspaces, last modified date (track this), a colored indicator dot. Clicking a password-protected project shows an inline password prompt. The default project (first) can be opened without password. Active project is highlighted.

Step 5 - Project Settings Tab: Provide options for the current active project: rename, change password, delete (with confirmation). Only show for non-default projects. Include a visual section for project metadata.

Step 6 - Export/Import Tab: Move the export/import functionality into this panel. Show two clear sections: 'Export All Projects' (single button that downloads the full JSON) and 'Import Projects' (file picker with format detection). Add a visual preview of what will be imported before confirming.

Step 7 - Polish and Animations: Add transition animations for tab switching (fade/slide), hover effects on project cards, focus states on inputs, and proper keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter/Escape). Ensure the panel looks premium with proper spacing, typography (font-semibold headings, text-sm body), and subtle shadows.
acceptance_criteria: Running `npm run build` passes with no errors
Ctrl+Shift+K opens the project panel (Ctrl+Shift+P no longer triggers it)
Triple-click on the app logo still opens the project panel
The panel has a tabbed/sidebar navigation with at least: New, Open, Settings, Export/Import sections
The New Project form has name input, password field with show/hide toggle, and validates before submission
The Open Project view shows all projects as cards with visual information (name, workspace count)
Project Settings allows renaming, password change, and deletion with confirmation
Export/Import is accessible from within the panel
The panel has professional animations and styling (backdrop blur, smooth transitions)
No settings buttons or project management UI is visible in the normal app interface
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm it succeeds with no errors
findings: All changes implemented in src/App.jsx. Build passes with npm run build. Key notes: (1) Keyboard shortcut changed from Ctrl+Shift+P to Ctrl+Shift+K, (2) Project panel modes changed from main/create/switch/delete/changePassword to new/open/settings/export, (3) Added Settings icon to lucide-react imports, (4) Added panelImportRef, handlePanelImport, confirmImport helper functions for the import preview flow, (5) Added state vars: projectDescInput, showPasswordField, importPreview, deleteConfirm, (6) Gate mode (isGate=true) forces the Open tab and hides close/New/Export/Settings tabs, (7) The createProject function now resets to 'open' mode after success instead of old 'main' mode.
